violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the hallway to kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

Forks and spoons in the cafeteria are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cabinet area. Single use parchment paper was observed with droppings on package. Parchment paper was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
The 3-bay sink faucet was observed leaking. Per PIC, 3-bay faucets will be fixed on 11/11/2025. New Violation. Correct By: 11/11/2025

The cabinet in the kitchen area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
The cabinet back wall was observed with an opening/hole. Establishment is to repair hole. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (washed apples) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
Chicken nuggets were observed delivered at 92 degrees F. PIC placed in hot box to reheat. PIC called caterer during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen cabinets. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The water faucet in the 3 bay sink area is in poor repair (dripping water). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024

The faucet in the 3-bay sink area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Inspector observed leaks at two hot water faucets during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024

The faucet in the 3-bay sink area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Inspector observed leaks at two hot water faucets during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
The kitchen is lacking a hand-washing sink, with only a 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 11/03/2023
